subject: Geography
title: The Tibetan Plateau
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Tibetan Plateau usable.

hook.text: The Tibetan Plateau averages 4,500 meters—higher than most mountain peaks.

buildup.text: It's called the 'Roof of the World' and covers an area the size of Western Europe.

discovery.text: Asia's mightiest rivers—the Yangtze, Mekong, and Indus—all start on this plateau.

twist.text: The plateau affects global weather. It deflects the jet stream and drives the monsoons.

climax.text: Its glaciers are melting fast, threatening water supplies for two billion people.

punchline.text: One plateau controls water for a third of humanity.

quiz.question: What is the Tibetan Plateau's average elevation?
